rag_rules: Place AGREE annex content in implementation scope when referencing implementation-level bindings or subcomponent ports.
Use only visible or explicitly declared feature names in assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Assumptions should constrain inputs using concrete, case-provided bounds or named constants.
Guarantees should express output behavior via boolean expressions over visible values and ranges.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'eq' to define helper boolean expressions for range checks: eq range_check : bool = value >= min and value <= max;
Ensure all variables in AGREE expressions are declared with types matching their AADL source (bool, int, real).
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type annexes without intermediate port mapping.
Avoid hardcoding literal types in expressions—align helper variable types with AADL port types.
